name: Affordable Debt Consolidation & Debt Relief Dallas
description_short: Texas-based debt settlement company negotiating with creditors to reduce unsecured debt. Charges 15% performance-based fees, positioning itself as lower-cost than national competitors.
description_long:
Affordable Debt Consolidation & Debt Relief Dallas is a debt settlement and negotiation company targeting Texas residents with $50,000 to $300,000+ in credit card and personal loan debt. The company positions itself as a specialized service for higher-income earners in Texas, offering what it calls a "Texas Debt Relief program" built around debt settlement and negotiation rather than traditional consolidation loans.

The company's primary service is debt settlement, where it negotiates directly with creditors to reduce total amounts owed. Funds are set aside in FDIC-insured accounts and deployed to settle accounts one at a time. They also offer affiliate referrals for debt consolidation loans through third-party lenders, credit counseling options, and connections to bankruptcy attorneys as a last-resort option. Free consultations with Texas debt specialists are available to assess eligibility and explain program mechanics.

Affordable Debt Consolidation differentiates itself primarily through fee structure, claiming a flat 15% performance-based fee on enrolled debt—charged only after successful settlement—versus industry standards of up to 25%. They emphasize being Texas-focused with dedicated specialists familiar with state-specific financial challenges. The company holds an A+ BBB rating with zero complaints according to their website and references media appearances to establish credibility.

A significant caveat is that the company cannot guarantee specific settlement percentages or outcomes, which it acknowledges is illegal under federal and Texas law. Monthly payments are described as potentially "less than half" of current credit card payments, but actual results depend on individual circumstances, creditor willingness to negotiate, and factors outside the company's control. The debt settlement approach itself extends the resolution timeline and may impact credit scores during the settlement period.
address: 1341 W Mockingbird Ln Suit 600W, Dallas, TX 75247

company_info.headquarters: 1341 W Mockingbird Ln Suit 600W, Dallas, TX 75247

pros: Charges 15% performance-based fees (flat rate, only after settlement), positioned as up to 40% lower than national competitors charging up to 25%
A+ BBB rating with zero complaints reported on their profile
FDIC-insured savings account for settlement funds, providing deposit security
Texas-specific focus with dedicated specialists familiar with state finances and creditor relationships
Free, no-obligation consultation with debt specialists to assess eligibility before enrollment
Performance-based model means no fees charged until debt is successfully negotiated and settled
Offers multiple pathways (settlement, counseling, consolidation loans, bankruptcy referrals) rather than one-size-fits-all approach
cons: Cannot guarantee settlement percentages or specific debt reduction outcomes, limiting predictability for consumers
Debt settlement approach typically extends resolution timeline compared to consolidation loans, keeping accounts in active negotiation longer
Settlement of debts for less than owed can negatively impact credit scores during the program
Requires stable monthly cash flow to fund settlement account, making the program unsuitable for those with irregular income
High debt minimum ($50K) excludes consumers with smaller debt burdens from their 'specialized' higher-income program
best_for: Texas residents with $50K-$300K in high-interest credit card and unsecured personal loan debt who want an alternative to bankruptcy
Higher-income earners with stable monthly income who can fund a settlement account while negotiating with creditors
Consumers who can tolerate a multi-year debt resolution timeline in exchange for potentially lower total payoff amounts
Texans seeking a local, state-focused debt company rather than national debt relief chains
diagnosis: Best for stable, higher-income Texas residents with $50K+ in unsecured debt who prefer negotiated settlement over new consolidation loans and can tolerate potential credit score impact during a multi-year resolution period. Primary caveat: debt settlement cannot guarantee specific payoff percentages, results vary widely by creditor, and the approach extends your debt resolution timeline compared to consolidation or bankruptcy alternatives.
services: Debt settlement and negotiation with creditors for credit cards and personal loans
FDIC-insured settlement savings accounts to hold funds for negotiated payoffs
Free no-obligation consultations with Texas debt specialists
Customized repayment plans tailored to individual budgets
Affiliate referrals for debt consolidation loan comparison across multiple lenders
Credit counseling services and interest rate reduction guidance
Referrals to bankruptcy attorneys for legal debt relief options
Monthly payment management and account resolution tracking
Performance-based fee structure (15% of enrolled debt, charged after settlement)
Higher-income earner program for $50K-$300K+ debt cases
quarantine_reason: Website hacked — serving cialis/spam content. Delisted 2026-04-01.
